{{Infobox character
| name = Wandering Titan
| appears_in = Cradle
| titles = Dreadgod
| abilities = hunger technique
| introduced = cradle-04-skysworn
}}

''See also: [[Wandering Titan Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The Wandering [[Titan]] is a [[Dreadgod]], an immensely powerful and catastrophic entity characterized by its inevitability rather than its speed. Often described as a force of nature, it is frequently depicted as a massive, turtle-shelled armored warrior with human-like features, such as five-fingered hands and a serpentine tail. Its physical presence is so immense that its shell can resemble an [[island]] or a rising hill, and its movements cause massive tectonic shifts, destroying landscapes and burying everything in its path. The [[Titan]] is known to feed on powerful materials, including minerals, [[aura]], and fragments of destroyed worlds. In combat, the Wandering [[Titan]] possesses overwhelming strength capable of upending the heavens and earth, straining the fabric of space, and challenging even the most powerful [[Monarchs]]. It has demonstrated tactical intelligence, such as switching places with other [[Dreadgods]] or using portals to travel. While it is considered one of the slowest [[Dreadgods]], its sheer scale and power make it a looming disaster for any civilization in its path. Various groups, such as the [[Abyssal Palace]], model their paths and techniques after the [[Titan]]'s perceived indestructibility.}}

== Skysworn ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a creature in a chasm on the ocean's floor that rolls its stone joints.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c18">''Skysworn'', chapter 18</ref>}}

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Wandering Titan]] is an image of a turtle-shelled armored warrior.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c10">''Uncrowned'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Wandering Titan]] was attacked by twelve [[Monarchs]] during the [[Dread War]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c15">''Uncrowned'', chapter 15</ref>}}

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Rumors began to circulate that the Wandering [[Titan]] was stirring, with its anticipated awakening expected in less than three months. This [[Dreadgod]], often depicted as a man-walking turtle, had moved to the edge of [[Akura]] territory with its shell forming a hill rising from the water and one arm reaching toward the land. As an entity that feeds on minerals and powerful materials, it serves as a model for the [[Path]] used by members of the [[Abyssal Palace]] and is associated with a yellow image representing its will. Although it is a location where [[Akura]] operations have occurred, the creature remains a threat that requires evacuations before it fully wakes. Ultimately, the Wandering [[Titan]] began waking earlier than expected, acting as a source of power for others and a being from which [[madra]] can be drained.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The awakening of the [[Wandering Titan]] is a significant upcoming event.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c2">''Wintersteel'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Wandering Titan]] is expected to rise in less than three months.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|There has been buzz about the [[Wandering Titan]] stirring.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3">''Wintersteel'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Wandering Titan]] has moved to the edge of [[Akura]] territory.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c5">''Wintersteel'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a location where an [[Akura]] operation is occurring.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c7">''Wintersteel'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a location.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9">''Wintersteel'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a [[Dreadgod]] whose shell has become a hill rising from the water.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a [[Dreadgod]] that has reached up to the land with one arm.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Wandering Titan]] is often depicted as a turtle that walks like a man.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Wandering Titan]] feeds on powerful materials and minerals.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a threat that people must be evacuated from before it wakes.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c12">''Wintersteel'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Dreadgod cults]] may be a danger to survivors of the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c13">''Wintersteel'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a yellow image associated with the [[Dreadgod]]'s will.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c14">''Wintersteel'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a model for the [[Path]] used by [[Abyssal Palace]] members.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c15">''Wintersteel'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The bear dreadbeast inherited power from the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c21">''Wintersteel'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a being from which [[madra]] can be drained.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c23">''Wintersteel'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a being that will eventually awaken.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c24">''Wintersteel'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Brother Aekin]] took on the aspect of the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c27">''Wintersteel'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Wandering Titan]] was waking early.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28">''Wintersteel'', chapter 28</ref>}}

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The Wandering [[Titan]] is a dark stone humanoid giant with rocky skin, a monkey-like tail, and a turtle's shell rising from its shoulders. As one of the four [[Dreadgods]], this inevitable source of chaos dominates the [[earth aura]] for hundreds of miles and uses a hunger technique to consume physical materials and power. During its approach, the entity's waxing power caused it to crash through a mountain and cut a swath of devastation across the landscape. While fighting a [[Monarch]], the massive creature broke portals and eventually arrived at [[Sacred Valley]] to search for the source of [[hunger madra]]. This force of nature, which can roar and swipe with massive limbs, presented a direct challenge to [[Lindon]] as [[Ziel]]'s group engaged it in combat.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a threat.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2">''Bloodline'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a threat that cloudship crews are ordered to flee from.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] is known for its inevitability.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4">''Bloodline'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a humanoid giant of dark stone that kneels at the edge of the ocean.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] has a turtle's shell rising behind its shoulders.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]]'s presence dominates the [[earth aura]] for hundreds of miles.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] uses a hunger technique to consume power and physical materials.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] has a tail resembling a monkey's tail.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] cycled its [[madra]] for a brief instant.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] is one of the four [[Dreadgods]] depicted in a mural.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c6">''Bloodline'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a source of chaos and [[Dreadgod]] influence.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c10">''Bloodline'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] is expected to arrive in one week.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] is approaching.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c12">''Bloodline'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]]'s power was waxing like a rising sun.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] crashed through a mountain.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c13">''Bloodline'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Yerin]] wants [[Northstrider]] to keep the [[Wandering Titan]] away.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c14">''Bloodline'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a massive entity that can roar.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c15">''Bloodline'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] roared after an arrow struck.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a threat that [[Malice]] would drive off.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c16">''Bloodline'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] has rocky skin.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] cut a swath of devastation across the landscape.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17">''Bloodline'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Kazan clan]] traveled during a fight between the [[Wandering Titan]] and a [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a massive entity that can break portals.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] turned back toward [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] has arrived at [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18">''Bloodline'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a challenge [[Lindon]] faces.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a force of nature comparable to a thunderbolt or an aura-storm.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] began drawing its hand back for a swipe.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19">''Bloodline'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Ziel]]'s group is fighting the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]]'s foot is covering the sky from [[Yerin]]'s perspective.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] was searching for the source of [[hunger madra]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c22">''Bloodline'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]]'s appetites from the [[Wandering Titan]] made him long to consume a mummified hand.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c22" />}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Having previously faced [[Lindon]] and his team during a period of weakness, the Wandering [[Titan]] later launched an attack on the town of [[Refuge]] where it shattered [[Mercy]]'s [[bloodline armor]]. During this conflict, the entity annihilated a chamber's guardian spirit and provided energy for [[Lindon]] to consume. The creature is so massive that its footsteps mimic the sound of the [[Eight]]-Man [[Empire]]'s war-bands, and its casual strikes are capable of triggering earthquakes. Throughout its existence, the [[Titan]] is driven by a hunger that constantly returns.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] and his team previously fought the [[Wandering Titan]] at its weakest.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c2">''Reaper'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The town of [[Refuge]] was attacked by the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4">''Reaper'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Wandering Titan]] shattered [[Mercy]]'s [[bloodline armor]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] consumed energy from the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c7">''Reaper'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Wandering Titan]] annihilated the guardian spirit of the chamber.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c8">''Reaper'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The war-bands of the [[Eight]]-Man [[Empire]] sound like the footsteps of the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c10">''Reaper'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Wandering Titan]]'s attacks can be casual.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c12">''Reaper'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Wandering Titan]] is capable of causing earthquakes.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c20">''Reaper'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Wandering Titan]] experiences hunger that always returns.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24">''Reaper'', chapter 24</ref>}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The Wandering [[Titan]] is a [[Dreadgod]] characterized as a towering black statue with stone-like skin and shell, featuring a serpentine tail and the ability to breathe gold [[dragon]]'s breath. Although it possesses chaotic golden madra, this entity is unable to attack minds and was previously weakened by a suppression field while remaining on [[Ashwind]]. After being seen in battle with [[Northstrider]], the creature attempted to cross a portal to attack [[Moongrave]]. As it makes its way toward [[Moongrave]], [[Blackflame City]] has been evacuated and all parties are preparing to face the entity, which serves as a reference for strength.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Wandering Titan]] was weakened by a suppression field.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3">''Dreadgod'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Wandering Titan]] remains on [[Ashwind]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c4">''Dreadgod'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Wandering Titan]] possesses chaotic golden madra.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5">''Dreadgod'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Northstrider]] was seen in battle with the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c6">''Dreadgod'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Wandering Titan]] attempted to cross a portal to attack [[Moongrave]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c8">''Dreadgod'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Everyone is preparing to face the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c12">''Dreadgod'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Wandering Titan]] is on its way to [[Moongrave]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a reference for strength.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c13">''Dreadgod'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a [[Dreadgod]] that cannot attack minds.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15">''Dreadgod'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Wandering Titan]] has a serpentine tail.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Wandering Titan]] can breathe gold [[dragon]]'s breath.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Blackflame City]] was evacuated before the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21">''Dreadgod'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Wandering Titan]] is a towering black statue with stone-like skin and shell.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21" />}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The Wandering [[Titan]] is an echo of [[hunger madra]] characterized by a single colossal eye and the ability to blink through space. During its existence, [[Lindon]]'s [[Dreadgod]] hand consumed its blood essence and vitality, while the entity also demonstrated the ability to swap places with the [[Silent King]]. In combat, the creature utilized a [[Striker technique]], momentum-providing punches, and its tail to maintain balance, though its tail eventually cracked into [[Lindon]]. Despite clashing with [[the Ghost]] and catching [[dragon]]'s breath on its shell, the Wandering [[Titan]]'s corpse has not yet been obtained by [[Lindon]], even though it is a being that can be fashioned into a hammer.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Mercy]] has experience facing a weakened [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c3">''Waybound'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|One of the [[Paths]] of Heaven displays a symbol that reminds [[Lindon]] of the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c4">''Waybound'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Orthos]] wants to be able to fight the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c10">''Waybound'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]]'s [[Dreadgod]] hand fed on the vitality and blood essence of the [[Wandering Titan]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c11">''Waybound'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Wandering Titan]] can blink through space.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c14">''Waybound'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Wandering Titan]] is an echo of [[hunger madra]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c15">''Waybound'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Wandering Titan]] swapped places with the [[Silent King]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c21">''Waybound'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Wandering Titan]] can be made into a hammer.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Wandering Titan]] has one colossal eye.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c24">''Waybound'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Wandering Titan]] uses its tail to catch its balance.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c27">''Waybound'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Wandering Titan]] uses a [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Wandering Titan]]'s tail cracked into [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c28">''Waybound'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Wandering Titan]] uses a punch that can provide momentum.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Wandering Titan]] is clashing with [[the Ghost]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c30">''Waybound'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Wandering Titan]] caught [[dragon]]'s breath on its shell.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c30" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] has not yet received the [[Wandering Titan]]'s corpse.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c33">''Waybound'', chapter 33</ref>}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Wandering Titan]] fought against [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c1">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Lindon]] has three shells hewn from the [[Wandering Titan]] orbiting his body.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Wandering Titan]] stepped through a portal to trade places with the [[Silent King]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c14">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 14</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|“The Wandering Titan has made his way to the edge of Akura territory. We go to protect the people and to drive away the vultures.”<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c5" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|“I want you to keep the Wandering Titan away from where it’s going.”<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c14" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle characters]]
